message:
fix(core): dirname("//") glibc parity, serialize rand48 tests, fnmatch doc polish

core/unistd/mod.rs (dirname // special case)
  - POSIX allows an implementation-defined interpretation of leading
    "//" (the so-called network-root form). Glibc treats the exact
    input `"//"` as a directory of itself and returns `"//"`, while
    collapsing every run of 3+ leading slashes to `"/"`. Our
    `dirname_range` previously stripped trailing slashes
    unconditionally and returned `(0, 0)` (→ `"."`) for `"//"`, which
    diverged from glibc and surfaced in the dirname conformance diff.
  - Add an explicit early-return: if `path == b"//"`, return (0, 2).
    All other slash-run collapsing continues to work as before.
  - Add `dirname_double_slash` unit test that locks in the `"//"`
    → `"//"` behavior so a future "simplify this" refactor doesn't
    silently re-break glibc parity.

core/stdlib/random48.rs (test-only serialization)
  - The `srand48`/`drand48`/`lrand48` family shares a single global
    48-bit state by design. Cargo runs tests in parallel threads by
    default, so tests that `srand48(seed); assert drand48()`… were
    flaky whenever another test concurrently reseeded the state
    between the seed call and the read.
  - Introduce a file-scoped `RANDOM48_TEST_LOCK: Mutex<()>` and a
    `random48_lock()` helper that expects un-poisoning. Each test
    takes the lock for its full duration. The lock is test-cfg only
    — it does not leak into the production code path.
  - No behavioral change; removes a source of intermittent CI noise
    around `test_srand48_deterministic`, `test_drand48_range`, and
    `test_lrand48_range`.

core/string/fnmatch.rs (doc-only cleanup)
  - Compact the module-level docstring and the `BracketShape` enum
    doc into short paragraph form that still calls out the three
    bracket-expression outcomes (Terminated / LiteralFallback /
    Invalid) and the glibc reference. Pure documentation polish,
    no code change.

core/search/linear.rs (rustfmt-only)
  - Rewrap the `checked_mul(...).map(...).unwrap_or(true)` guard to
    conform to the workspace rustfmt width. No logic change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
